content:
# Gpr

**Summary**: A geophysical method to image the subsurface structure using electromagnetic pulses.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[2022-argadestya|Argadestya, 2022]] used GPR to interpret sedimentary structures in shoreface and dune environments.

- [[2024-saraih|Saraih, 2024]] used GPR to analyze burrow attributes like morphology and size in the Aruma Formation.

## Instruments and Software



- [[2024-saraih|Saraih, 2024]] used GPR to analyze burrow attributes like morphology and size in the Aruma Formation.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- Revealed upslope climbing clinoforms with an angle of repose indicating wave influence. ([[2022-argadestya|Argadestya, 2022, p. 71]])

- GPR data provided information on burrow attributes (morphology, size, and percentage) that cannot be obtained from CT scans or outcrop photographs. ([[2024-saraih|Saraih, 2024, p. 20]])
